page.title: Billing
page.subtitle: Balance, top-up, and documents for legal entities
personalAccount.label: Personal account number
invoice.ourOrganization.hint: Legal entity issuing the invoice. Defaults to the organization assigned to the client.
invoice.subject.label: Service description
invoice.subject.hint: You can override wording for this invoice only. Leave as suggested to use the default nomenclature.
documents.title: Documents
organization.form.subject: Service name for documents
organization.form.subjectHint: Leave empty to use the server default nomenclature.
organization.form.lookupSuccess: Details loaded by TIN. Please verify.
organization.form.lookupFailed: Could not load details by TIN. Enter manually.
organization.form.innHint: Enter 10 digits for a legal entity or 12 for sole proprietor — details load automatically.
organization.form.kppHint: KPP is required for legal entities when multiple branches match or lookup fails.
organization.form.kppRequired: Enter KPP (9 digits) for legal entities.
organization.form.chooseBranch: Multiple organizations match this TIN — select one:
organization.form.lookupRequiresKpp: TIN alone is not enough. Enter KPP below.
organization.form.connectEdoHint: The counterparty must accept the invitation in their EDO operator (Diadoc, Saby, etc.) before you can send invoices via EDO.
organization.form.edoParticipantIdHint: Enter the ID from your EDO operator's portal (Diadoc, Saby, etc.). It is not filled automatically from TIN lookup.
organization.form.edoCheckHint: Enter the counterparty EDO ID first. The check searches SBIS; for Saby partners not listed in invitation changes, SBIS is queried directly (an existing route usually returns Connected immediately).
organization.form.edoCheckProbePending: Route is not active yet: SBIS sent an invitation; waiting for acceptance
organization.form.edoCheckRequiresId: Enter the EDO identifier before checking
organization.form.edoCheckConnected: EDO is connected — you can send invoices via EDO
organization.form.edoCheckPending: Invitation found — waiting for counterparty acceptance
organization.form.edoCheckNotFound: No active route found. The client can send an invitation, or use the SBIS portal
billing.explainer.title: How billing works
billing.explainer.model: Prepaid balance with pay-as-you-go usage charges.
billing.explainer.prepaid: You top up in advance in rubles; your balance is credited in US dollars at the exchange rate at the time of payment.
billing.explainer.payg: Services (AI Assistants, AI Analytics, STT, etc.) are charged from your balance as you use them; reports show cost in ₽ at the rate when each charge was recorded.
billing.explainer.displayRub: Your current balance is shown in rubles at the current exchange rate.
billing.explainer.topupRub: When topping up, enter an amount in rubles; before payment you will see how much USD will be credited to your balance at the current rate.
billing.explainer.paygShort: Usage is charged from your prepaid balance as it occurs.
billing.topup.rubHint: Enter the top-up amount in rubles. The equivalent in US dollars will be credited to your balance.
billing.usage.hint: Costs in the table are in rubles at the rate when each charge was recorded; the ledger balance is in USD.
limits.title: Balance thresholds and alerts
limits.description: You can set a balance threshold. When balance falls below it, we will email you and, if needed, issue an invoice automatically.
limits.emailsHint: Separate multiple addresses with a comma, semicolon, or space.
limits.noOrganizations: Create an organization on the Organizations tab to enable automatic invoicing.
limits.averageMonthlyHint: When the threshold is reached, the invoice amount will be based on average spend over the last 30 days.
limits.tenantUsers.label: Tenant users
limits.tenantUsers.empty: No users with email in this tenant.
